title: Buttery Silky Saudi Arabian Chicken Curry
description: A rich and creamy Saudi Arabian chicken curry dish featuring tender chicken, buttery sauce, and a blend of aromatic spices, perfect for a monsoon evening. This egg-free-friendly recipe is a delight to the senses.
instructions: In a large pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it's translucent and starting to caramelize, about 8 minutes.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook until it's browned on all sides and cooked through, about 6-8 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the pan and set it aside.
In the same pan, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter.
Once the butter has melted, add the bhuna spice mix and curry powder. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the spices are fragrant.
Pour in the coconut milk and chicken broth, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and let cook for 5 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Return the chicken to the pan and stir to coat with the sauce.
Season with salt to taste, then garnish with chopped cilantro.
